text:
The Chimera Ant Egg (, Kimera Anto no Tamago) is an item that was up for auction at a venue hosted by the Mafia Community. It only appears in the Hunter × Hunter: The Real 4D movie attraction at Universal Studios Japan.

Appearance

The Chimera Ant Egg appears green, with the silhouette of the creature visible from the outside. It is preserved in a state of suspended animation and contained within a storage tank.

Background

The auctioneer states that this Chimera Ant Egg came from the Dark Continent.

Plot

USJ The Real 4-D Attraction

It is the first item for auction at an auction hosted by the Mafia Community. The egg is described as a hybrid of an unknown creature and is considered exceptionally repulsive, even among other Chimera Ants. It is also warned that the creature could awaken if it were to receive even a small impact. When the remaining items are introduced, the auction begins, and the starting bid for the egg is 5 billion and quickly rose to the final bid of 15 billion.

A deep voice can be heard voicing their disappointment regarding the transaction. They state that an item as amazing as this should be under the ownership of someone that is at least equal to it in value. They go on to say that if there is no such person, they will take it for themselves. The voice is revealed to belong to Hisoka, who takes over the auction. He announces that he will have the audience keep him entertained until his other toys arrive and begins to attack them with his cards.

A chant for Jajanken is heard. Gon, Killua and Kurapika emerge from the explosion. Gon apologizes, saying that the items were stolen and that they were sent by the Hunter Association to retrieve them. Hisoka uses Bungee Gum to take the Chimera Ant Egg hostage while he fights the three Hunters. During the battle, the auctioneer panics as the egg continuously receives an impact. It finally begins to hatch after receiving a shock from Gon's Paper attack. The Chimera Ant is capable of speech upon hatching.

Gon and the others engage the Chimera Ant in battle. After a while, the Ant takes note of the Scarlet Eyes and asks Kurapika if they are important to him. After it is revealed that Kurapika is the last surviving member of the Kurta Clan he uses Emperor Time and continues to fight the Ant, declaring he will have it regret its actions. The situation becomes dire, and Gon risks his life to protect everyone in the venue with one last Jajanken attack to defeat the Ant.
